commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a regression test and changes timeout/overflow compaction in `run.ts` to use the pending live `/model` selection's provider/model instead of the run-start provider/model.

PR surface: Source +9, Tests +107. Total +116 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: current main sets `requestedSelection` from a pending live `/model` switch, but timeout and overflow compaction still use stale run-start context when the attempt cannot restart cleanly. I did not run a live multi-provider compaction session in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Hold for contributor-visible code repair and real behavior proof; ClawSweeper should not queue a repair because automation cannot provide the external contributor's real setup proof.

target.title: fix(agents): keep auto-compaction on the live session model (#95696)
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes #95696. After a mid-session `/model` switch, the UI shows the new model and normal turns use it, but when context compaction triggers (timeout recovery or overflow recovery) the compaction call silently reverts to the model that was active when the run started. Subsequent compactions keep using the stale model.

## Why This Change Was Made

The auto-compaction sites in `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.ts` fed the run-start-resolved `provider`/`modelId` (set once during model resolution, lines ~1025/1124) into `buildEmbeddedCompactionRuntimeContext`. A live `/model` switch is persisted to the session store and picked up elsewhere, but those frozen locals were never refreshed, so compaction targeted the pre-switch provider.

The run loop already resolves the current selection one block earlier as `requestedSelection = shouldSwitchToLiveModel(...)` (the restart check). When a switch is pending but the attempt cannot restart in place (mid-turn work already happened, so `canRestartForLiveSwitch` is false), it falls through to compaction. This change reuses that already-resolved selection as the compaction provider/model, falling back to the run-start values when no switch is pending. No new store read is added on the path; it reuses the prepared selection the loop already computed.

## User Impact

After switching models with `/model`, compaction now uses the model you selected, not the one you started the session with. No behavior change when no switch is pending.

## Evidence

- **Behavior addressed:** Auto-compaction (timeout and overflow recovery) used the stale run-start model instead of the live `/model` selection persisted in the session store.
- **Real environment tested:** Vitest against the real `shouldSwitchToLiveModel` + `buildEmbeddedCompactionRuntimeContext` modules (only the session-store I/O and the registry-validating model-ref resolver are mocked, at the same boundary `live-model-switch.test.ts` uses). New file `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compaction-live-model-override.test.ts`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compaction-live-model-override.test.ts`; `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compaction-live-model-override.test.ts`; `oxfmt --check`.
- **Evidence after fix:** With a persisted override (`liveModelSwitchPending` + `providerOverride: openai` / `modelOverride: gpt-5.5`) and a run-start model of `anthropic`/`sonnet-4.6`, the resolved compaction runtime context is `provider: openai`, `model: gpt-5.5`. With no override persisted, it stays `anthropic`/`sonnet-4.6`.
- **Observed result after fix:** 2 passed. oxlint exit 0, format clean.
- **What was not tested:** A full live multi-provider gateway session driving real timeout/overflow compaction end-to-end (no local multi-provider credentials). The regression test drives the exact composition the run loop uses at both compaction sites; the wiring change itself is the 2-line reuse of `requestedSelection` at those sites.
